comment_text.sn29.3:1.3: Dragons who wished to be reborn as a human were encouraged to observe the sabbath (Kd 1:63.4.3, Ja 506), during which they would cast off their serpent form (vossaṭṭhakāyā) and appear human (snakes are experts at skin-shedding). This seems to have been misunderstood in later days to imply that they should literally sacrifice their bodies, a belief unsupported in early Buddhism (Ja 524).
comment_text.sn29.3:2.2: “Both kinds of deeds” (dvayakārino): both good and bad. Contrast the good deeds for being reborn as a centaur (SN 31.2).

root_text.sn29.3:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn29.3:1.2: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn29.3:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena m’idhekacce aṇḍajā nāgā uposathaṁ upavasanti vossaṭṭhakāyā ca bhavantī”ti?
root_text.sn29.3:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekaccānaṁ aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn29.3:2.2: ‘mayaṁ kho pubbe kāyena dvayakārino ahumha, vācāya dvayakārino, manasā dvayakārino.
root_text.sn29.3:2.3: Te mayaṁ kāyena dvayakārino, vācāya dvayakārino, manasā dvayakārino, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapannā.
root_text.sn29.3:2.4: Sacajja mayaṁ kāyena sucaritaṁ careyyāma, vācāya sucaritaṁ careyyāma, manasā sucaritaṁ careyyāma, evaṁ mayaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjeyyāma.
root_text.sn29.3:2.5: Handa mayaṁ etarahi kāyena sucaritaṁ carāma, vācāya sucaritaṁ carāma, manasā sucaritaṁ carāmā’ti.
root_text.sn29.3:2.6: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena m’idhekacce aṇḍajā nāgā uposathaṁ upavasanti vossaṭṭhakāyā ca bhavantī”ti.

translation_text.sn29.3:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn29.3:1.2: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn29.3:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why some egg-born dragons keep the sabbath, having transformed their bodies?”
translation_text.sn29.3:2.1: “Mendicant, it’s when some egg-born dragons think:
translation_text.sn29.3:2.2: ‘In the past we did both kinds of deeds by body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn29.3:2.3: When the body broke up, after death, we were reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons.
translation_text.sn29.3:2.4: If today we do good things by body, speech, and mind, when the body breaks up, after death, we may be reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn29.3:2.5: Come, let us do good things by way of body, speech, and mind.’
translation_text.sn29.3:2.6: This is the cause, this is the reason why some egg-born dragons keep the sabbath, having transformed their bodies.”
